item.title: 1 dead, 3 hurt in high school graduation shooting in Northern California
item.description: One person died and three others, including a child, were injured in a shooting Wednesday night at a high school graduation in Northern California, officials said.

item.title: Actor James Handy fatally stab by his girlfriend's son, officials say
item.description: Actor James Handy, famous for his roles in "Top Gun: Maverick" and "Jumanji," was stabbed to death at a home in the Tarzana neighborhood of Los Angeles on Wednesday, officials say. His girlfriend's son, Michael Gledhill, was arrested on suspicion of murder. CBS News' Adam Yamaguchi reports.

item.title: Hilton, Becerra lead California governor's race as Steyer faces narrowing path
item.description: Steve Hilton, Xavier Becerra and Tom Steyer have emerged as the leading contenders to advance to November's general election as vote counting continues.

item.title: California governor, LA mayoral races still unsettled as vote count continues
item.description: Outcomes in key races for governor and LA mayor remain unsettled as mayoral hopefuls Spencer Pratt and Nithya Raman, as well as gubernatorial candidates Xavier Becerra, Steve Hilton and Tom Steyer await their electoral fates.

item.title: Israel-Hezbollah fighting casts cloud over prospects for U.S.-Iran peace deal
item.description: It remains unclear how close the U.S. and Iran are to striking a deal to end the Iran war amid continued hostilities between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah.

item.title: Graham Platner pushes back against disturbing allegations from women he once dated
item.description: Democratic Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner is reacting to a report by "The New York Times" outlining some of his apparent behavior in past romantic relationships. CBS News' Fin Gómez reports.

item.title: Financial conversations to have with your partner before getting married
item.description: As wedding season gets underway, CBS News business analyst Jill Schlesinger joins "CBS Mornings" to break down the financial conversations every couple should have before getting married.

item.title: Flesh-eating screwworm fly is reemerging in Texas after decades, threatening livestock
item.description: A flesh-eating parasite called the screwworm fly has reemerged in Texas. It's the first time in decades the parasite, which can kill livestock, has threatened the U.S. cattle industry. Jason Allen reports.

item.title: American journalist detained in Iran sounds alarm from prison, pleads for medical help
item.description: Iranian-American journalist Reza Valizadeh is speaking out in a recorded phone call from inside Iran's Evin Prison, making a plea for the U.S. government to obtain medical help for him and the other Americans detained by Tehran. "Face The Nation" moderator Margaret Brennan reports.

item.title: Parents of college student missing in Japan say "we will find him"
item.description: It's been one week since American college student James "Weston" Higginbotham disappeared in Japan while vacationing with his family. His parents spoke to Anna Coren about the search and why their son was spotted getting on a train alone.

item.title: Employers added 172,000 jobs in May, surging past expectations
item.description: The labor market continues to show strength despite rising inflation and concerns about slowing economic growth.

item.title: Senate passes massive ICE funding bill, avoids ban on "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: The Senate's 18-hour-long "vote-a-rama" reaped a big win for President Trump after Republicans passed a massive ICE funding bill and avoided codifying a ban against the Justice Department's "anti-weaponization" fund. CBS News' Aaron Navarro reports.

item.title: Senate passes reconciliation bill to fund ICE for 3 years, without DOJ fund ban
item.description: Senate Republicans passed funding for the Department of Homeland Security's immigration enforcement agencies following a "vote-a-rama." The measure didn't ban the administration's "anti-weaponization" fund.

item.title: Graham Platner faces new allegations just days ahead of Maine primary
item.description: Democrat Graham Platner, who is running for Senate in Maine and would face Sen. Susan Collins if he wins the upcoming primary, is facing new allegations. The New York Times reports some women who previously dated Platner say they saw disturbing behavior. Platner denies the allegations.

item.title: Senate passes bill to fund ICE for 3 years, without a ban on the "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: The Senate passed a $70 billion reconciliation package to fund ICE through the end of President Trump's term. The legislation, which heads to the House, did not include a ban on the president's "anti-weaponization" fund. Nancy Cordes reports.

item.title: Eye Opener: Maine Senate candidate faces more allegations ahead of primary
item.description: A Democratic Senate candidate from Maine faces more allegations of misconduct just days before voters head to the polls. Plus, the latest on the stabbing death of actor James Handy. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.

item.title: Trump's border czar denies inhumane conditions at ICE facility: "The spaghetti was good"
item.description: President Trump's border czar Tom Homan defended conditions inside Delaney Hall, a detention center in Newark, New Jersey. Delaney Hall has been the target of multiple protests since Memorial Day weekend and many held inside have called the conditions "brutal and inhumane."

item.title: Actor James Handy allegedly stabbed to death by girlfriend's son
item.description: Actor James Handy has died at the age of 81 after police say he was found with stab wounds. The son of Handy's girlfriend has been charged in the killing. Adam Yamaguchi reports.

item.title: Homan defends conditions at N.J. ICE detention center: "The spaghetti was good"
item.description: In a CBS News interview, White House border czar Tom Homan defended conditions at the Delaney Hall ICE detention center, amid intense protests over the New Jersey facility. "

item.title: First officer told captain of United Airlines flight that hit light pole he was "slow and a little low," NTSB says
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board released its preliminary report on a United Airlines plane that struck a light pole on the New Jersey Turnpike in May.

item.title: Meet the Belmont Stakes horses running in the 2026 race
item.description: The Belmont Stakes will host a New York rematch of the top two finishing horses from the Kentucky Derby​ to wrap up horse racing's Triple Crown for 2026.

item.title: Listen to journalist Reza Valizadeh's voice memo from Iran's Evin Prison here
item.description: CBS News obtained a brief voice memo from Iranian American journalist Reza Valizadeh, who is being detained in Iran's Evin Prison and is pleading for help for him and other American captives.

item.title: Actor James Handy allegedly killed by girlfriend's son at LA home
item.description: 44-year-old Michael Gledhill was arrested on suspicion of murder after he turned himself in, according to police.

item.title: Appeals court reverses former paramedics' convictions in McClain death
item.description: The Colorado Court of Appeals reversed the criminally negligent homicide convictions for the former paramedics in the death of Elijah McClain.

item.title: Full interview: Tom Homan on New Jersey ICE facility allegations, World Cup, more
item.description: Trump border czar Tom Homan speaks with CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez about the Delaney Hall ICE detention center in New Jersey, ICE's security obligations during the World Cup, potential enforcement operations in New York and more.

item.title: What borrowers need to consider before the July federal student loan changes
item.description: Borrowers have a short window to prepare for numerous upcoming student loan changes. Here's where to start.

item.title: Meet the horses who ran in the Preakness Stakes
item.description: The Preakness Stakes featured its biggest field in 15 years with 14 horses in the middle jewel of horse racing's Triple Crown.

item.title: Kentucky Derby winner skipping Preakness Stakes, won't make Triple Crown run
item.description: The last time a horse won the Triple Crown was in 2018.

item.title: Golden Tempo wins 152nd Kentucky Derby, making history for its trainer
item.description: Cherie DeVaux became the first woman to train the winner of the opening leg of the Triple Crown.

item.title: Meet the Kentucky Derby horses who ran in the 2026 race
item.description: The Kentucky Derby saw a field of 18 horses Saturday in the first leg of the 2026 competition for horse racing's Triple Crown.
